violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety that is registered with the RIDOH Center for Food Protection. The lead kitchen staff personnel member has been certified in food safety and is to be registered with the RIDOH Center for Food Protection as the Establishment Registered Food Safety Manager as soon as possible. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2026

Grab and go items packaged in the food establishment are lacking complete label information. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients (sub-ingredients and allergens), the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Labels are to be adjusted to include the sub-ingredients and allergens. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site person certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Email sent to Chartwells area Manager regarding need for Food Safety Certified individual at the time of inspection. Kitchen personnel to be registered for food safety course as soon as possible. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety that is registered with the RIDOH Center for Food Protection. A kitchen staff personnel member is to be certified in food safety and registered with the RIDOH Center for Food Protection as the Establishment Registered Food Safety Manager as soon as possible. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of staff, corn salsa (cooked corn and beans mixed with salsa) was placed hot in the 2 door reach-in refrigerator in a plastic wrap covered container. Per the PIC, the corn salsa is intended for same day lunch service. To ensure proper cooling and cold holding at time of service, salsa was loosely covered and placed in the walk-in freezer to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/22/2025
No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC washed, rinsed and sanitized during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2025
Two large cans were observed inside of a hand washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2024

At the time of inspection, a box of frozen food stored below the ceiling/wall mounted fan unit, was observed to have an accumulation of ice on it. Food items not to be stored directly below the fan unit due to unknown source of leak/dripping causing the ice build-up. Walk-in Freezer unit to be monitored for leaks/dripping. New Violation. Correct By: 09/26/2023

At the time of inspection, the 3 bay sink in the kitchen area was observed to be constructed in a way that allows for water drainage directly to the floor below at times of washing dishes/utensils. Non-FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be free of unnecessary ledges, projections, and crevices, and designed and constructed to allow easy cleaning and to facilitate maintenance. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2023

The condensate from the Cold Holding Well on the food service line was observed to be dripping/draining onto the shelf below (no tubing was observed to be connected for proper conveying of liquid wastes/condensate). Condensate drainage and other non-sewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2023

The grout lines to the tile below the dish machine and 3 bay sink area have an accumulation of soil residue/mildew. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2023
